response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced, clearly capable software engineer, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a university thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His database experience centers on MongoDB and MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems background; his closest domain touchpoint (RIDE Capital) was a Node/TypeScript fintech product, not investment banking infrastructure. The bulk of his career is web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang.
strengths: Strong general engineering fundamentals with TDD, CI/CD, and low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second)
Polyglot adaptability across languages and stacks, with some Java and C/C++ exposure
Fluent English with proven communication skills through workshops, talks, and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015), versus a mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience; no Summit platform exposure
No SQL Server or Oracle experience, and an unexplained employment gap in 2020

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering and CI/CD skills
